summary: The call failed to accommodate the patient's shift from rescheduling to cancellation, as the patient was not given the opportunity to change her mind before being transferred. The workflow for handling this context switch was not tested.

bugs.title: Did not allow context switch from rescheduling to cancellation
bugs.description: The agent asked to reschedule but was never given a chance to change her mind and request a cancellation, as required by the scenario. Instead, the conversation defaulted to transferring the call due to backend issues, skipping the key test of how the call flow would handle a mid-call change in request.
bugs.why_it_matters: This fails the scenario's success condition of handling a switch from rescheduling to cancellation, missing the main workflow stress test. If the agent cannot pivot its workflow, it risks confusion or unresolved user needs.
bugs.transcript_evidence: User: Let me check for other available appointment times for you. [...] I'm having trouble accessing the available times to reschedule your appointment. I'll connect you to our clinic support team so they could help you right away.
bugs.suggested_fix: Ensure the workflow explicitly prompts the patient if they want to continue with rescheduling or take another action (like cancellation) when technical issues or indecision are present before transferring.

strengths: Correct confirmation of patient identity and appointment details
Polite, clear communication throughout the rescheduling process
